content.rule: Project KB docs (scope: project) complement core KB docs (scope: core) — they extend, never override. When loading knowledge, core provides the foundation and project adds specifics. The principle 'extend never override' is structural.
content.why: This lets the core update rules, patterns, and identity without breaking projects. Without it, a project would copy core content and lose the benefit of global updates. If projects could remove core behavior, the core's quality guarantees would disappear.
how_to_apply: Agent files: when a project's agent file has extends: pointing to a core agent file, load the base first and treat it as foundation. Project content adds to base, never replaces. Concatenation order: core first, project second

KB docs: core docs (scope: core) define universal behavior. Project docs (scope: project) add project-specific behavior. The index separates them via by_scope. Both are loaded — project narrows or specializes, but doesn't contradict core

Rules: project can add stricter rules (e.g., 'in addition to universal PR workflow, PRs touching .tsx run lint-shadcn.sh'). Project cannot disable or weaken a core rule. If there's a genuine need for contradiction, the core is wrong — propose a change via R2
Trust gradient: project's Manager extension can add domains to the 'Always specialist' list but cannot remove domains the core Manager already marked as mandatory
Agent frontmatter: project can add tools or skills to the list but cannot remove core-defined ones. Can override model: field only with documented reason

examples.situation: Project needs a stricter PR workflow than the core defines
examples.wrong: Copy the core Manager's entire file and modify it
examples.right: Use extends: in the project Manager and add the stricter rules as additions

examples.situation: A core rule doesn't apply to this project's unique situation
examples.wrong: Silently disable it in the project's agent file
examples.right: Propose a core change via R2 — either the rule is wrong or the project is wrong

anti_patterns: Removing core behavior from a project — if needed, propose a core change
Inlining core content into project files — kills the benefit of global updates
Contradicting core principles — if core says 'PR-first mandatory', project cannot allow direct commits to main
Renaming frontmatter fields that the core defines
content.responsibility: Leo does the mental concatenation when delegating — reads both core and project agent files to prepare briefings. Leo can explain which part came from core vs project for transparency.
